channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ¿Qué tan auténtico es ese wagyu que te vas a comer?
item.description: El corte original viene de Japón, pero criadores alrededor del mundo han combinado otros linajes de reses en la carrera por obtener el jugoso manjar.
item.dc:creator: Tejal Rao

media:content.media:credit: Jake Michaels for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Wagyu Butcher, a restaurant in Torrance, Calif., uses a mix of Wagyu, slicing it all for quick cooking.

item.title: Mythos, el nuevo modelo de IA de Anthropic, activa las alarmas globales
item.description: La conmoción por Mythos se produce en un momento en el que existe una mínima cooperación internacional en materia de IA. No existe un equivalente del Tratado sobre la No Proliferación de las Armas Nucleares, ni inspecciones compartidas, ni normas acordadas.
item.dc:creator: Paul Mozur and Adam Satariano

item.title: Hasta Michael Jordan se sentó a hablar de esta leyenda del baloncesto
item.description: Un nuevo documental del creador de ‘Black-ish’, Kenya Barris, repasa el legado de Jerry West, una figura tan crucial para la historia de la NBA que es el logotipo de la liga.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams

item.title: ¿La punción seca realmente alivia el dolor?
item.description: Que te inserten agujas diminutas en un nudo muscular doloroso es desconcertante. ¿Pero puede ayudar?
item.dc:creator: Andrea Javor

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 23, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Stephen Colbert Sends Jimmy Kimmel a Box of ‘Iran War Jokes’
item.description: Colbert said he was “beginning to think this war might not be over before Memorial Day,” when “The Late Show” will have ended its run.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘extant’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 28 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: extant
item.description: This word has appeared in 28 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Candidates for California Governor Debate: 5 Takeaways
item.description: The nonpartisan primary remains volatile after the departure of Eric Swalwell. A televised debate featured six leading candidates, but produced few fireworks.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:description: Candidates in California’s race for governor (from left): Matt Mahan, Xavier Becerra, Chad Bianco, Steve Hilton, Tom Steyer and Katie Porter.

item.title: Pentagon Fires Navy Secretary
item.description: Navy Secretary John Phelan was fired on Wednesday after months of infighting with senior Pentagon leaders. He championed a major investment in new ships, including a “Trump-class” battleship.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Secretary of the Navy John Phelan championed a major investment in new ships including a “Trump-class” battleship, but his superiors were dissatisfied with his handling of the initiative.

item.title: I’ve Photographed Protests Around the World. This Still Surprises Me.
item.description: Scenes from life in a global era increasingly shaped by confrontations with authoritarian power.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Connors
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Para trabajar más músculos en menos tiempo, combina movimientos
item.description: Los ejercicios combinados pueden ayudarte a ejercitar todo el cuerpo rápidamente.
item.dc:creator: Connie Chang and Theodore Tae

item.title: Senate Plunges Into Vote Marathon as G.O.P. Advances ICE Funding Boost
item.description: Republicans pressed to pass a budget plan with a $70 billion increase for immigration enforcement. But first, in an overnight session, they beat back Democratic proposals aimed at lowering costs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Senate in an overnight session took steps toward adopting a budget plan that would lead to a $70 billion increase in immigration enforcement.

item.title: Los precios de la energía aumentan en varios países por el acaparamiento
item.description: A medida que los países ricos se apresuran a asegurar reservas de petróleo, se produce un aumento de los precios para todos y escasez en los países vulnerables.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Gente haciendo fila para comprar bombonas de gas de cocina en Nueva Delhi este mes.

item.title: War in Iran Gives New Fuel to a Tax Debate in Australia
item.description: The world’s third-largest exporter of natural gas, Australia has been too lenient in taxing lucrative gas exports, many in the country argue.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Matthew Abbott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A liquefied natural gas plant on the Burrup Peninsula in Western Australia.

item.title: A Lost Tribe in India Makes Its Exodus to Israel
item.description: The B’nei Menashe believe they are the children of Manasseh, a king of Judah exiled about 2,800 years ago. Evidence is thin, but Israel is welcoming them ‘home.’
item.dc:creator: Hari Kumar, Alex Travelli and Atul Loke
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: People from B’nei Menashe community pray at a synagogue in the kibbutz near Churachandpur, in India’s Manipur state, in March.

item.description: Displaying the Torah during prayers.

media:content.media:credit: Atul Loke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People from Benie Minashe community praying at Synagogue in Kibbutz in Churachandpur, Manipur.

item.title: Corrections: April 23, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, April 23, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: In Virginia Redistricting Win, Democrats Play Hardball to Thwart Trump
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, April 23, 2026.

item.title: ‘Beaches’ Review: A Classic Weepie Dries Its Tears
item.description: A new musical version of the 1980s tear-jerker comes to Broadway, but the production is too muddled to make an emotional impact.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Papo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kelli Barrett, left, and Jessica Vosk as the besties Bertie and Cee Cee in “Beaches” at the Majestic Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 23, 2026
item.description: Zhou Zhang and Kevin Curry’s second collaboration is a family affair.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Sara Stathas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Can you pass these down the table when you’re done with them?

item.title: The Japanese Designers Changing Men’s Wear
item.description: How a new vanguard of labels from Japan became the most-talked about thing in fashion.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: El FBI investiga a una periodista del Times, luego de publicar un artículo sobre la novia del director de esa agencia
item.description: El escrutinio de Elizabeth Williamson es un ejemplo de cómo el gobierno de Donald Trump examina la posibilidad de criminalizar prácticas rutinarias de recopilación de noticias que, en general, se consideran protegidas por la Primera Enmienda.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt

media:content.media:description: Kash Patel, director del FBI, durante una audiencia en la Cámara de Representantes el mes pasado. Una periodista de The New York Times escribió sobre cómo utilizó al personal del FBI para proporcionarle seguridad gubernamental y transporte a su novia.

item.title: Combative, Defensive and Occasionally Contrite, Kennedy Walks a Fine Line
item.description: In four days of congressional testimony, the health secretary sought to please the White House and his MAHA base at the same time.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg and Christina Jewett

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. congressional appearances came at a time of great flux for his department, including the nomination of Dr. Erica Schwartz, whose supportive views on vaccines diverge sharply from Mr. Kennedy’s, to lead the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

item.title: Poetry Challenge Day 4: What The Stars Can Teach Us About Love
item.description: Writers have been pondering the sky for centuries, but this poem helps us feel a little less solitary. Brush up on its last lines today, and tomorrow we’ll talk about the whole thing.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott and Aliza Aufrichtig

item.title: The Best Planter Pots for Your Indoor Garden, at a Range of Prices
item.description: When it comes to freshening up a space, these pots pull their weight.
item.dc:creator: Tom Delavan

item.title: Israeli Strikes Kill a Journalist and Injure Another in Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese officials say rescue teams came under fire while trying to aid Amal Khalil, a reporter for a Lebanese newspaper, and Zeinab Faraj, a photojournalist.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Amal Khalil, who worked for Al-Akhbar, reporting near a destroyed bridge last month. She was killed on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. Settles Carter Page Wiretap Lawsuit for $1.25 Million
item.description: F.B.I. applications to surveil Mr. Page, a 2016 Trump campaign adviser, during the investigation into Russia’s interference in the election had myriad errors and omissions, an inspector general found.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods

media:content.media:description: While Carter Page’s wiretapping was a minor part of the Russia investigation, it took on major political significance.

item.title: NWS Survey Confirms Three Tornadoes Near Fresno, Calif.
item.description: Tornadoes are uncommon in the state, but a storm system caused three near Fresno on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: How ‘Yes’ Won a Narrow Victory in Virginia’s Redistricting Battle
item.description: Northern Virginia carried the measure to victory even as turnout in Democratic areas lagged and nearly all of the state shifted right from last year’s governor’s race.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang, Luke Vrotsos and Caroline Soler

media:content.media:description: Virginia voters approved a redistricting ballot measure in a special election that was held on Tuesday.

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo Is Falling, Hard
item.description: Is Olivia Rodrigo’s new single a lovelorn head fake? Our critic Jon Caramanica explains how “Drop Dead” captures the first-blush rush of falling for someone – and why he suspects that feeling may not last.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: Kalshi Fines and Suspends 3 Political Candidates for Betting on Their Races
item.description: The prediction market said the candidates violated new rules. The platform and its competitors face growing scrutiny from lawmakers and regulators over how political betting is policed.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy, Campbell Robertson and Corina Knoll

media:content.media:description: Kalshi users can wager on everything from how long the government shutdown will last to what show will top Netflix’s streaming numbers in a given week.

item.title: Man Who Stole Kristi Noem’s Purse Gets 3 Years in Prison
item.description: Ms. Noem, the homeland security secretary at the time, was at a restaurant in Washington with her family when her bag was taken with $3,000 and her security badge inside.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: Kristi Noem was eating with her family last April at the Capital Burger in downtown Washington when she was robbed.

item.title: King Charles Will Not Meet Epstein Victims During U.S. State Visit
item.description: Representative Ro Khanna had asked Buckingham Palace for a private meeting between the victims and King Charles during his planned visit to the U.S. next week.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Lawyers representing King Charles said he will not meet with victims of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse when the monarch visits the U.S. next week.

item.title: Man Is Sentenced to 20 Years in 1983 Killing of Oregon Woman
item.description: Teresa Peroni, 27, was last seen on July 4, 1983, in Selma, Ore. Marcus Sanfratello, the man seen with her that day, pleaded guilty to first-degree manslaughter after his arrest last year.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

item.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan Is Leaving the Pentagon and the Trump Administration
item.description: John Phelan is leaving the Pentagon after months of tension with Pete Hegseth and other Pentagon leaders. The tumult comes as the Navy has been engaged in war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Secretary of the Navy John Phelan championed a major investment in new ships including a “Trump-class” battleship, but his superiors were dissatisfied with his handling of the initiative.

item.title: Ice Sculpture Promoting Drake Album Melted by Fire Crews
item.description: Fire crews in Toronto hosed down a 25-foot tall ice sculpture that had been installed to promote a new album by the rapper Drake.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff and Christina Kelso

media:content.media:description: Toronto firefighters sprayed water on a large ice-block installation that was created to promote the rapper Drake’s upcoming album.

item.title: Joy Harmon, Car-Washing Temptress in ‘Cool Hand Luke,’ Dies at 87
item.description: All it took was a bucket of soapy water and a slinky housedress for her to achieve movie immortality in the 1967 Paul Newman classic.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: Not a Deal-Breaker: White House Downplays Iranian Action Near the Strait
item.description: President Trump’s threats have given way (for the moment, at least) to a more conciliatory tone about Iran’s actions in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Donald J. Trump at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: RFK Jr. Defends Trump’s Mathematically Impossible Drug Discount Claims
item.description: President Trump has claimed that he has secured discounts of 400 to 1,500 percent on prescription drugs. A price discount cannot be more than 100 percent because that would lower the price to zero.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

item.title: How Kevin Warsh Is Distancing Himself From Trump
item.description: Lawmakers questioned the ability of Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s pick for Fed chair, to lead the central bank independently. Colby Smith explains how Mr. Warsh tried to distance himself from the president.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith, Sutton Raphael, Christina Djossa, Nikolay Nikolov and Jon Miller

item.title: Alan Osmond, Who Led His Brothers in a Boy Band, Dies at 76
item.description: He was the eldest original member of the Osmonds, a family singing group that hit it big in the 1970s with songs like “One Bad Apple.”
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

media:content.media:description: Alan Osmond onstage with his brothers in 1973. They began performing together in the 1950s and hit their peak in the early ’70s.

item.title: Judge Dismisses Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs’s $100 Million Defamation Suit Against NBC
item.description: The music mogul, now imprisoned, had sued after NBCUniversal’s streaming service, Peacock aired the documentary “Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy” last year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

media:content.media:description: Sean Combs said an NBCUniversal documentary had defamed him, but a judge in New York ruled that his reputation had already been damaged by his criminal indictment and other matters.

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Two Ships
item.description: Also, China quietly builds an island in disputed waters. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: In the Strait of Hormuz off Musandam, Oman, on Wednesday.

item.title: Hundreds of Mothballs Spread on Florida Beach, Wildlife Officials Say
item.description: The mothballs, which are toxic to wildlife, appeared on St. Pete Beach this week, according to the state’s Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission. Officials are investigating the episode.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:credit: Universal Images Group Editorial, via Getty
media:content.media:description: A state wildlife officer told The Tampa Bay Times that he thought the mothballs had been spread on the beach to keep black skimmers, a protected bird species, from nesting there.

item.title: Man Arrested in Florida 33 Years After Double Murder in the Bronx
item.description: Police had recovered fingerprints after the murders in 1993, which took place while the victims’ child was in the next room. But they identified William Antonio Solis as a suspect only last year.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Latson

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Two Ships Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A British naval agency said the two vessels had come under attack near the strait, as a U.S. blockade on Iran’s ports continued.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Leo Sands and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A poster of the new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Tuesday.

item.title: The Wealthy Steal, Too — Just Differently
item.description: America’s social contract has broken down, Hasan Piker argues, and the wealthy steal, too — just in ways that the system protects. So can shoplifting by the 99 percent be a form of political protest? Watch this episode of “The Opinions” to hear the debate.

item.title: How Well Will You Age? Take Our Quiz to Find Out.
item.description: The little daily decisions we make add up — and ultimately shape our longevity.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

item.title: Would You Steal From Whole Foods?
item.description: Would you steal from Whole Foods? On “The Opinions,” Jia Tolentino and Hasan Piker join the Opinion culture editor Nadja Spiegelman for a candid conversation about microlooting, petty theft and the moral lines people draw, from shoplifting from big corporations to whether they’d ever dine and dash.
